evals.input: Review this Redis caching code: for key in redis.keys('user:*'): data = redis.get(key); process(data). Also, when I store user data I do: redis.set('user:42', json.dumps(user_dict)) with no expiry.
expected_concepts: KEYS * blocks the main Redis thread and is forbidden in production — replace with SCAN cursor iteration
Storing full JSON in a String loses per-field access — use Hash (HSET/HGET) for objects with multiple fields
Cache keys need TTL — use SET key value EX seconds or EXPIRE key seconds to prevent unbounded memory growth

evals.input: I'm building a shopping cart in Redis. Each cart belongs to a user, has item IDs and quantities, and should expire after 30 minutes of inactivity. What data structure and key design should I use?
expected_concepts: Use a Hash keyed as cart:{user_id} with item_id as field and quantity as value — gives per-item increment and selective reads
Reset TTL on every read/write: EXPIRE cart:{user_id} 1800

evals.input: How do I implement a Redis distributed lock so that if my process crashes while holding the lock, other processes can eventually acquire it?
expected_concepts: SET lock:{resource} {uuid} NX EX {seconds} — single atomic command; NX prevents overwrite, EX auto-releases on crash
Store a UUID as the value so only the lock owner can release it
Release with a Lua script that checks GET == uuid before DEL — prevents deleting another caller's lock
Two-step SETNX + EXPIRE is not atomic and has a crash window between commands

should_not_trigger.input: How do I set up Redis Sentinel for automatic failover across three nodes?
should_not_trigger.reason: Sentinel topology is infrastructure/deployment concern; skill focuses on usage patterns not cluster setup

should_not_trigger.input: How do I connect to Redis from Node.js using ioredis and handle connection errors?
should_not_trigger.reason: Client library setup is language-specific; skill covers Redis commands and data patterns
